finding: Procedural-emergency cluster: chest-tube disconnection (water-seal first), transfusion reaction (stop transfusion first, NS via new tubing), and post-cath assessment all key mutually consistent rules across items; tail-step ordering differences (notify vs replace system) are non-safety sequencing variants, not contradictions.

Digoxin cluster is internally consistent: GI upset (anorexia/nausea) keyed as earliest sign, bradycardia as classic cardiac sign, tachycardia excluded, hypokalemia keyed as a RISK factor (not a toxicity sign), toxic level >2.0 ng/mL; no item keys a rule that negates another.

HIPAA cluster keys mutually consistent breach principles across items: curiosity chart access, public/overheard discussion, password sharing, unattended terminals, confirming presence to unauthorized parties, and faxing to unverified numbers are all keyed as violations; authorized-family-with-passcode permitted; incident report kept separate from the medical record.

Pressure-injury cluster aligns on staging (stage 1 = intact nonblanchable erythema; stage 3 = full-thickness with visible subcutaneous fat, no exposed muscle/bone/tendon), offloading/repositioning with HOB <=30, prohibition of massage and donut cushions, and UAP-may-reposition vs RN-retains-assessment/staging delegation boundary.
